LangGraph is a library for building stateful, multi-actor applications with LLMs.
It is used to create agent and multi-agent workflows.
LangGraph is built on top of LangChain and extends its capabilities.

Key features of LangGraph include:
- Cycles and branching: Implement loops and conditionals in your apps.
- Persistence: Automatically save state after each step in the graph.
- Human-in-the-loop: Interrupt graph execution to approve or edit next action.
- Streaming support: Stream outputs as they are produced by each node.
- Integration with LangChain: Seamlessly works with LangChain and LangSmith.

LangGraph was designed to address common challenges when building complex AI applications.
It provides a clear way to define control flow and data flow in agentic applications.
The library uses a graph-based approach where nodes represent actions and edges represent transitions.
